evaluatorContextNote: This report supersedes the prior self-evaluation (commit cb1f0fe) that was produced from the generator's own context. Each criterion below was independently re-verified by running fresh Python subprocesses, the pytest suite, and headless CLI invocations against the sprint-02 branch.

criteriaResults.evidence: `python -c "import whisperdeep.generator"` succeeds and resolves to whisperdeep/generator.py. Module exposes generate() and DungeonGenerator.
generate(80, 40, 12345) returns a Floor with .width=80, .height=40 and 14 rooms. Tile access at (0,0) and (79,39) returns 'wall' as expected (border).
Two calls to generate(60,30,42) produce snapshot() tuples that compare equal (full grid). Determinism confirmed.
SHA1 hashes of snapshots for seeds {1,2,3,4,5} -> 5 unique hashes (>= 4 required).

generate(80,40,12345) -> 14 rooms; pairwise interior-tile-set intersection check yielded 0 overlaps across all 91 room pairs. Room.overlaps uses padding=1 (floor.py:44) preventing wall-touching adjacency.
BFS over walkable tiles from arbitrary seed reaches 1162/1162 walkable tiles on seed=12345. Connectivity verified by tests/test_generator.py::test_all_walkable_tiles_reachable across multiple seeds.

Across seeds {1,2,3,4,5}: average 28.4 doors per floor (>=1 required). For every door, at least one orthogonal neighbor is room interior AND >=2 neighbors are walkable. Zero adjacency violations. Door placement (generator.py:172-226) only marks corridor tiles flanked by walls perpendicular to the corridor axis.

World(master_seed=999, num_floors=3): floor0 up=0/down=1, floor1 up=1/down=1, floor2 up=1/down=0. Exactly matches contract rule. Driven by World.get_floor's place_upstairs/place_downstairs flags (world.py:62-63).
Stairs are placed at room centers (generator.py:110, 122), guaranteeing they sit on floor tiles. Connectivity (C6) ensures every walkable tile, including stair tiles, is reachable from any other.
Game._choose_spawn returns rooms[0].center (game.py:43-49). Verified: spawn at (74,29) with seed=42, tile kind 'floor', position lies inside a room rectangle.
Teleporting onto floor0.downstairs_pos and calling descend() returns True; current_floor_index becomes 1; player tile becomes 'upstairs'. game.py:73-92.
After descend, calling ascend() returns True; current_floor_index returns to 0; player tile is 'downstairs'. game.py:94-112.

snapshot() of floor 0 before descend equals snapshot() after descend+ascend round-trip. World caches Floor instances in self._floors dict (world.py:48,59-72) so the same Floor object is returned on revisit.
World(master_seed=999, num_floors=3) generates all 3 floors; SHA1 hashes of floor 0 vs floor 1 differ. derive_floor_seed (world.py:17-30) uses bitwise avalanche mixing to ensure distinct seeds.

tiles._GLYPH_BY_KIND maps wall->#, floor->., door->+, upstairs-><, downstairs->>; Player.glyph='@'. Independently confirmed by instantiating each Tile kind and reading .glyph. tests/test_game.py::test_render_uses_documented_glyph_set passes.
`python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless` produces a 40x80 frame containing '#', '.', '@', '>', and visually recognizable enclosed rooms with corridors and doors. Frame inspected directly.

Iterating all 8 directions from spawn and attempting to move into wall directions: try_move returns False and player position is unchanged in every case. game.try_move (game.py:52-71) checks tile.walkable before mutating position.
Found a door at (58,5) on seed=1. Teleporting player to adjacent walkable tile (59,5) and issuing move(-1,0) returns True; player ends at (58,5) on tile of kind 'door'. tiles._WALKABLE includes DOOR.
`python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless` invoked twice produces byte-identical output; `--seed 8 --headless` output differs. argparse wires --seed -> World(master_seed=...) at cli.py:74-76.

pytest collected and passed 21 tests: tests/test_generator.py (11 tests targeting determinism, dimensions, rooms, connectivity, doors, stairs, multi-floor world) and tests/test_game.py (10 tests for spawn, descend, ascend, persistence, rendering, walls, doors, CLI). Generator-targeted tests count is 11, well above the >=5 threshold.

Constructing World+Game succeeds without error; render_frame returns a non-empty string containing '@'; calling try_move((1,0)) from default spawn returns True (free direction). Per handoff.json, sprint 1 was bootstrapped together with sprint 2; the regression registry has no prior sprint-1 criteria, so this in-contract regression check is the sole sprint-1-equivalent gate and passes.

docs/whisperdeep.md documents: (a) tunable generator parameters (max_rooms, room_min_size, room_max_size, max_placement_tries, place_upstairs, place_downstairs) in a table; (b) the complete glyph legend (#, ., +, <, >, @) in a table; (c) --seed CLI flag with default and behavior in 'Command-line flags' section. All three required topics covered.

regression.note: Contract lists regressionSprints: [1] but harness-state/regression/registry.json has no registered sprint-1 criteria (sprint 1 was bootstrapped as part of sprint 2). C21 in this contract serves as the equivalent regression check and passes.

correctness.rationale: All 22 acceptance criteria independently verified. 21/21 pytest tests pass. Generator is correct and deterministic; connectivity is total; door placement is conservative but valid.

codeQuality.rationale: Clean module separation (tiles/floor/generator/world/game/render/cli/entity). Type hints, dataclasses, and docstrings throughout. Floor.snapshot() is a thoughtful test affordance. Room.overlaps uses 1-tile padding to prevent wall-piercing.

completeness.rationale: All deliverables present: generator module, World abstraction, render loop, --seed CLI flag, automated tests (21 of them), README/docs covering params/glyphs/--seed, and conventional commits ([C1] through [C22] tagged across two sprint commits).

robustness.rationale: Bounds checks with descriptive IndexError on Floor.get/set. Defensive fallback in descend/ascend if upstairs_pos/downstairs_pos missing. Single-room corner case handled in stair placement. Negative dimensions raise ValueError.

documentation.rationale: docs/whisperdeep.md is comprehensive: quick start, glyph legend, controls, generator API + tunable params, multi-floor world example, full CLI flag table, project layout. Module docstrings reinforce the docs.

scopeAdherence.inScopeDelivered: Rooms-and-corridors generator, doors, stairs, multi-floor descent/ascent with persistence, --seed flag, automated tests, documentation.
scopeAdherence.note: No FOV, combat, items, monsters, save/load, or LLM features added. Sprint 2 stayed within scope.

summary: Sprint 2 (Dungeon Generation v1) PASSES all 22 contract criteria with strong margin under independent verification. Generator is deterministic (same seed -> identical Floor; 5/5 distinct hashes across seeds 1-5), produces 14 non-overlapping rooms on 80x40 floors, full BFS connectivity (1162/1162 walkable tiles reachable on seed 12345), correct stair counts per floor (0/1, 1/1, 1/0 for a 3-floor world), persistent floors across descent/ascent (snapshot equality after round-trip), wall collisions respected, doors traversable as walkable tiles, and the --seed CLI flag is end-to-end deterministic (byte-identical headless frames for matching seeds). 21/21 pytest tests pass. Documentation covers all three required topics. No regression issues. No blocking failures.
recommendation: Merge harness/game-sprint-02 into harness/game and tag sprint-02-complete. Consider populating harness-state/regression/registry.json with sprint 2's most invariant criteria (C3 determinism, C6 connectivity, C8 stair invariants, C13 floor persistence, C19 --seed determinism) before sprint 3 begins so future evaluators can run regressions automatically.
